D-EXPLAN-001.decision: Lock experimentation plan (Phase A done, B->C->D->E->F; E-007 cryptarithm_guess prioritized).
D-EXPLAN-001.rationale: Cross-validated against winner repo (R13), discussion deltas (R11b), 18 ledger hypotheses. See EXPERIMENTATION_PLAN.md.

E-001-corpus-v1.notes: Assemble corpus.jsonl from 7 landed solvers + verified CoT; stratified train/dev; winner build_datum mask convention.

E-002-baseline-rank32.notes: Winner-faithful baseline. rank=32, alpha=64, 1 epoch, LinearDecay 2e-5->1e-5, Mamba+attention targets. Goal ~0.85.

E-006-select2reason.notes: Full corpus -> top-utility filtered (~30 percent retained).

E-007-cryptarithm-guess.notes: Add cryptarithm_guess CoT to corpus (winner skipped). Highest-confidence direct LB.

E-009-synthesis.notes: Combine winning ablation choices into final config. Train + submit (gated).

E-PRECOND-mamba-targets.notes: Smoke run (10 steps) on 2xT4 4-bit with explicit Mamba+attention targets. Validates no MoE-scan hang.

H-1EPOCH.statement: 1-epoch SFT is sufficient given high-quality CoT; more epochs over-fit on synthetic patterns.

H-BIT-NICKNAME.statement: Bit-manipulation problems use nickname encodings (e.g. "the integer formed by bytes…"); a robust regex extractor across all 1602 bit_manipulation rows yields 100% solver coverage.

H-CIPHER-100.expected_effect: +0.16 LB from solving all 1576 cipher rows in train + matching test distribution
H-CIPHER-100.statement: Cipher-category problems reduce to substitution+offset and admit a 100% deterministic solver.

H-CRYPTARITHM.statement: Cryptarithm-deduce problems are uniquely solvable letter-digit assignment problems; constraint propagation (or python-constraint / Z3) yields the exact answer.

H-CRYPTARITHM-GUESS.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB direct from a category the winner never trained on.
H-CRYPTARITHM-GUESS.statement: Winner skipped cryptarithm_guess; adding a solver + verified CoT for it gives direct LB headroom (164 train rows).

H-CURRICULUM.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB vs random ordering, free improvement
H-CURRICULUM.statement: Difficulty-ordered curriculum SFT (easy→hard within each category) generalizes better than random shuffle on the hidden test set.

H-DQ.statement: DATA QUALITY is the dominant lever: per-category deterministic solvers + verified  oxed{} CoT corpora beat fancier training regimes.

H-EQ-FALLBACK.statement: Default to absolute-difference fallback in equation_numeric solvers when no rule is found; captures edge cases.

H-EQ-SYMPY.statement: Equation-numeric problems are solvable with SymPy linear/quadratic solvers; algebraic CoT is reproducible.

H-GRAVITY-100.expected_effect: +0.17 LB from solving all 1597 gravity rows (16.8% of train)
H-GRAVITY-100.statement: Gravity-category problems (free-fall/projectile/orbital mechanics) reduce to closed-form physics formulas and admit a deterministic solver.

H-LOGPROB-FILTER.statement: Filtering out examples whose base-model min-logprob > -0.05 prevents over-tuning on easy examples and improves accuracy on hard rules.

H-LORA32.statement: LoRA rank 32 / alpha 64 SFT (winner config) is the right anchor; deviations should justify themselves with CV evidence.

H-MAMBA-TARGETS.expected_effect: Unblocks training on free GPU. Critical precondition.
H-MAMBA-TARGETS.statement: Explicit Mamba mixer LoRA targeting (mixer.in_proj, mixer.out_proj) + attention-only avoids the MoE expert-scan hang and enables training on 2xT4 4-bit.

H-NORL.statement: RL / distillation are NOT needed; pure SFT on verified CoT reaches top-LB.

H-NUMERAL-100.statement: Numeral-system problems (base conversions, radix arithmetic, novel digit systems) are deterministic given the rules in the prompt.

H-RANK16.statement: LoRA rank 16 (half the cap) reaches within 0.005 of rank-32 with half the parameters; ablation worth running for compute efficiency.

H-SELECT2REASON.expected_effect: ~equal LB at half cost; may slightly improve generalization.
H-SELECT2REASON.statement: Select2Reason-style curation (drop low-utility / repetitive traces) matches baseline accuracy at ~30 percent of cost.

H-UNIT-CONV-100.statement: Unit-conversion problems decompose into a directed graph of conversion factors; a Floyd-Warshall closure over the parsed factors yields exact answers.

TASK-4.0.gpu: no  (authoring only — training runs on the GPU executor)
TASK-4.0.title: Author the QLoRA training script + Kaggle kernel

TASK-E-PRECOND-mamba-targets-smoke.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-smoke-mamba/. Change LoRA target_modules to the EXPLICIT list ['mixer.in_proj','mixer.out_proj','q_proj','k_proj','v_proj','o_proj'] (NOT a regex). Set max_steps=10 (smoke only). Keep rank=32, alpha=64, 4-bit. Update kernel-metadata.json. The goal is to prove peft.get_peft_model() does NOT hang on the MoE expert scan on 2xT4. Offline test that asserts target_modules is a list of literal strings (no regex). Document the result expected: training loop starts within 30s of model load.
TASK-E-PRECOND-mamba-targets-smoke.title: Smoke-train kernel (10 steps) validating Mamba mixer LoRA targeting

TASK-E001-corpus-v1.spec: Build data/corpus/v1/build_corpus.py that walks the 7+ landed solvers in data/solvers/<category>/, calls each to generate verified prompt+CoT+answer triples, writes corpus.jsonl in the winner's build_datum format (tokens, mask aligned per references/analysis-winner-training-internals.md): mask=1 ONLY on the CoT-reasoning + boxed-answer span, mask=0 on the prompt. Stratified 90/10 train/dev split keeping category frequencies. Emit corpus_index.jsonl listing per-row id/category/token_counts. Tests: format validation, mask correctness, stratification quality.
TASK-E001-corpus-v1.title: Assemble corpus.jsonl v1 from landed solvers (winner build_datum convention)

TASK-E002-baseline-kernel.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-baseline-e002/. Final config: LoRA rank=32, alpha=64, 1 epoch, LinearDecayLRSchedule 2e-5 -> 1e-5 (per winner-lr_schedule.py), AdamW weight_decay=0.01, BF16 mixed precision, Mamba+attention target list from E-PRECOND, grad-accum for the largest stable micro-batch on 2xT4 4-bit, eval at end of epoch using eval/cv.py, write adapter/ + cv_score.json on success. Loss objective: maximize min log-prob over the masked CoT+answer span per winner-loss_config.py (CrossEntropyLossConfig baseline; no clipping/branch-weighting). Reject if smoke fails. Offline test that asserts rank<=32 and 1 epoch.
TASK-E002-baseline-kernel.title: Finalize winner-faithful baseline training kernel (rank32/alpha64/1-epoch/LinearDecay)

TASK-E003-rank16-finalize.spec: Sync kernels/train-rank16/ with E-002's config (LR schedule, target list, loss, data path) but with rank=16, alpha=32. Update kernel-metadata.json. Verify rank-16 via the existing assertion test (rejects rank 64 still passes). One change vs E-002.
TASK-E003-rank16-finalize.title: Align rank-16 ablation kernel (R24) with the E-002 config except rank/alpha

TASK-E004-2epoch-kernel.spec: Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-2epoch-e004/. Single change: total_epochs=2. Keep LinearDecayLRSchedule's denominator updated accordingly. Test asserts epochs=2.
TASK-E004-2epoch-kernel.title: Clone baseline kernel with epochs=2 (refutation test for H-1EPOCH)

TASK-E005-curriculum-wire.spec: Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-curriculum-e005/. Replace random shuffle of corpus.jsonl with the difficulty-ascending sort from data/curation/curriculum.py (R23). Offline test asserts the corpus order matches the curriculum config.
TASK-E005-curriculum-wire.title: Wire R23 curriculum config into the baseline kernel (difficulty-ascending)

TASK-E006-select2reason.spec: Add data/curation/select2reason.py that scores each corpus row by utility (trace length / category novelty / dedup-fingerprint), keeps top ~30 percent. Then clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-select2reason-e006/ pointing at the curated subset. Tests: dedup correctness; stratification preserved; subset size.
TASK-E006-select2reason.title: Select2Reason curation: keep top-utility ~30 percent of corpus, then train baseline

TASK-E007-cryptarithm-guess-integration.spec: CRITICAL PRIORITY: the winner skipped cryptarithm_guess (164 train rows). Use the cryptarithm solver from TASK-R28 to add verified CoT for cryptarithm_guess to the corpus (extend data/corpus/v1/build_corpus.py with the new category). Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-e007-cryptarithm/ pointing at the extended corpus. Tests: cryptarithm_guess rows present + verified.
TASK-E007-cryptarithm-guess-integration.title: Integrate cryptarithm_guess solver into corpus (winner skipped this category) — priority

TASK-E008-logprob-filter.spec: Add data/curation/logprob_filter.py: inference-pass the BASE model over corpus, record per-row min token log-prob, drop rows where min_logprob > -0.05. Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-logprob-filter-e008/ pointing at the filtered corpus. Document the dropped-rows histogram by category in the PR.
TASK-E008-logprob-filter.title: Pre-filter corpus by base-model min log-prob (drop already-easy examples)

TASK-E009-synthesis-kernel.spec: Operator runs this only after E-003..E-008 results are in. Build kernels/train-synthesis-e009/ combining the ablation choices whose CV beat E-002 (read state.json experiments collection for cv_score). Each combined choice cited in the kernel README. This is the final training run before finalize.
TASK-E009-synthesis-kernel.title: Synthesis: combine winning ablation choices into final config

TASK-K0-kaggle-access-smoke.spec:
Verify Jules has Kaggle access wired via the VM env vars. Run (capturing stdout):
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py whoami
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py submissions nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py leaderboard nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge
Write the THREE results (status + first 30 lines of body each) into competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/references/kaggle-access-smoke.md. Then in the PR Summary state whether kaggle_user is correct, today/cap (5), and our current LB position. NEVER print or commit KAGGLE_API_TOKEN / KAGGLE_USERNAME values themselves.
TASK-K0-kaggle-access-smoke.title: Kaggle access smoke test from the Jules VM

TASK-R1.supersedes_notes: legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
TASK-R1.title: Derive the problem-category taxonomy + classifier

TASK-R1-category-taxonomy.supersedes_notes: split per R-005 1h rule → TASK-R1a + TASK-R1b

TASK-R10.gpu: no   (authoring only; trains later on kaggle_gpu / local_40g)
TASK-R10.supersedes_notes: legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
TASK-R10.title: Author the QLoRA SFT training kernel (GPU-ready; runs later)

TASK-R10a-train-script-and-smoke.spec: Port winner's train_sft.py to a clean training entrypoint; assert rank<=32/alpha 64 at runtime; --smoke path runs on a toy model (no GPU); writes adapter/ + cv_score.json. Includes offline test that rejects rank=64.
TASK-R10a-train-script-and-smoke.title: QLoRA train.py + --smoke offline test (rank<=32/alpha 64 asserted)

TASK-R10b-kernel-metadata-readme.spec: kernel-metadata.json: GPU enabled, base model + dataset attached as placeholders; README documents the push->poll->pull workflow for the kaggle_gpu backend.
TASK-R10b-kernel-metadata-readme.title: Kaggle kernel-metadata.json + README for the training kernel

TASK-R11.supersedes_notes: legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
TASK-R11.title: Meta-analysis of the 217 discussion threads

TASK-R11a-discussion-do-avoid.spec: Skim all 217 discussion-*.md; produce a ranked 'do' list + 'avoid' list with discussion-id citations and short evidence per item.
TASK-R11a-discussion-do-avoid.title: 217 discussions → ranked Do / Avoid digest (cited)

TASK-R11b-deltas-and-new-hypotheses.spec: Using R11a + winner files, write a short deltas section and ≥5 concrete candidate hypotheses (statement, expected effect, how to test) ready for the operator to create_hypothesis.
TASK-R11b-deltas-and-new-hypotheses.title: Deltas vs winner + ≥5 new hypotheses for the ledger

TASK-R13-winner-trio-digest.spec: Read references/winner-lr_schedule.py.md, winner-loss_config.py.md, winner-train_common.py.md; produce ONE coherent digest covering LR schedule, loss/regularization choices, and the shared training utilities — each with file/line citations and how we replicate it in TASK-R10a.
TASK-R13-winner-trio-digest.title: Trio digest: winner LR schedule + loss config + train_common (clubbed)

TASK-R1a-category-taxonomy-doc.spec: Derive the category taxonomy with definitions + train.csv frequencies + value ranking (frequency × expected gain). DOC only, no classifier code.
TASK-R1a-category-taxonomy-doc.title: Category taxonomy + value ranking (doc only)

TASK-R1b-category-classifier.spec: Implement classify(prompt)->category + offline tests; report classification coverage on train. Depends on R1a's taxonomy.
TASK-R1b-category-classifier.title: Rule classifier + tests for the taxonomy

TASK-R2.<cat>.supersedes_notes: legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
TASK-R2.<cat>.title: Per-category deterministic solver  (TEMPLATE — operator clones one per category)

TASK-R20-cipher-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/cipher/solve.py (substitution+offset detection from prompt, exact answer) and generate.py (fresh cipher problems with known answers). Offline tests: report accuracy on 100 cipher rows from train; document any sub-types not yet handled. Goal: 100% solve rate on solvable instances.
TASK-R20-cipher-solver.title: Cipher-category deterministic solver + 100-sample holdout validation

TASK-R21-equation-sympy-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/equation_numeric/solve.py using SymPy: parse the equation system from prompt, solve(deduce: solve for the asked variable; guess: enumerate up to N candidates and verify). Offline tests on 50 deduce + 30 guess rows. Document failure modes.
TASK-R21-equation-sympy-solver.title: Equation-numeric SymPy solver + verification on holdout

TASK-R22-bit-manipulation-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/bit_manipulation/solve.py: detect bit nicknames (32/64-bit byte-form descriptions), reconstruct the integer, apply the requested operation, return exact answer. Test on 100 rows from train. Goal: 100% on solvable.
TASK-R22-bit-manipulation-solver.title: Bit-manipulation deterministic solver via nickname regex extraction

TASK-R23-curriculum-config.spec: Add data/curation/curriculum.py: per-row difficulty score (length, category fingerprint, solver-confidence), sort training data easy->hard, expose as a config option the training kernel can opt into. Offline tests on a small synthetic batch.
TASK-R23-curriculum-config.title: Difficulty estimator + curriculum-shuffle SFT config

TASK-R24-rank16-ablation-kernel.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-rank16/, change config to LoRA rank=16/alpha=32, update kernel-metadata.json. Verify --smoke path runs; existing rank<=32 assertion still passes (16<=32).
TASK-R24-rank16-ablation-kernel.title: Rank-16 LoRA ablation: kernel-metadata variant + smoke test

TASK-R25-gravity-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/gravity/solve.py: parse the physics prompt (find v0/g/h/angle/m/etc), apply the appropriate closed-form (kinematic eqs, projectile, orbital), return exact answer ( oxed{}). Also generate.py producing fresh gravity problems with known answers. Offline tests on 100 train rows from the gravity category. Target 100% on solvable.
TASK-R25-gravity-solver.title: Gravity-category deterministic solver (closed-form mechanics) + 100-sample validation

TASK-R26-numeral-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/numeral/solve.py: parse digit alphabet + base from prompt, convert input numeral to/from decimal, perform asked arithmetic, return exact answer. generate.py for synthetic numeral problems. Offline tests on 100 train rows.
TASK-R26-numeral-solver.title: Numeral-system deterministic solver (arbitrary radix + novel digit systems)

TASK-R27-unit-conversion-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/unit_conversion/solve.py: parse the unit-to-unit conversion factors from the prompt into a graph, run Floyd-Warshall transitive closure for the asked source->target, apply to the asked value, return exact answer. generate.py with synthetic conversion problems. Tests on 100 train rows.
TASK-R27-unit-conversion-solver.title: Unit-conversion deterministic solver via Floyd-Warshall conversion graph

TASK-R28-cryptarithm-solver.spec: Implement data/solvers/cryptarithm/solve.py: parse letter equation from prompt, search letter-digit assignments via constraint propagation (use python-constraint if available, else hand-rolled backtracking; Z3 acceptable but heavier dep), return the unique assignment. Offline tests on 50 deduce + 30 guess rows. Document any pathological cases.
TASK-R28-cryptarithm-solver.title: Cryptarithm-deduce solver via constraint propagation (python-constraint / Z3)

TASK-R29-eval-per-category-breakdown.spec: Extend eval/cv.py (from R8): add per-category accuracy breakdown using the taxonomy classifier (from R1b once landed; until then use a simple keyword router as fallback). Stratified holdout split keeping category balance. Produce a Markdown report with per-category accuracies.
TASK-R29-eval-per-category-breakdown.title: Extend CV harness to report per-category accuracy + stratified split

TASK-R7.dependencies: TASK-R1 (taxonomy), TASK-R2.* (solvers)  [start design now; wire to solvers as they land]
TASK-R7.title: Verified `\boxed{}` chain-of-thought generator

TASK-R8.title: Local CV eval harness mirroring host scoring
